transcript: Uh, this is Mr. Freeman’s? This is Elmer with—
Correct, sir. Yes. Hi, my name’s Elmer Whip with United Parcel Service. Our driver’s been out to your place a couple times this past week and no one answered the bell on a COD delivery.
Oh! COD? Did you—? F—who’s it from?
W—uh, you mind tellin’ me what the, uh, issue has been up there, sir? [inhales, exhales]
—Uh, I think you should talk to my wife. She’ll probably b—have more information.
Oh, I see! I see, okay.
Uh, yeah, my name’s Elmer Wh—
Yes, Miss. I’m Elmer Whip with United Parcel Service—
—the buzzer when our driver has been out there—
—Yeah, well, I mean, do I have to tell you—?
Sweetheart, I have to tell you you dialed the wrong number.
No, this is for the Freemans. Yes, I am aware of the number. I’ve worked here for 18 weeks now; I’ve been on the job months now. Okay?—
Well, I—you know, I’m on my way up the ladder.
We don’t have anything COD, okay? So send it—
—It’s, uh—I’ve got a lot of magnifying glasses here for Mr. Julius Freemans, okay?
Well, that’s not the correct name, so, therefore, I’m telling you this is a fictitious phone call and you can try the next number on your list, okay, Elmer?—
—Lemme just get a credit card number and then we’ll leave it in a safe place for ya, okay?—
—Ye—and you know what—? Do you—do you speak Italian? Do you speak Italian?—
—I’ll teach you some: [unclear, perhaps, "Fancul o mazza e soreta!" or "Mancu co o mazza toie!"]
Yes, sir. This is UPS.com calling. How are we today?
We’re fine! How are you, UPS.com?
Uh, doin’ well, sir. We just got a COD here. Our driver was out there and no one answered the buzzer. We’ve got a carton of magnifying glasses—
—Well then send him back! Then—then—then—then your driver—
—He’ll be there within the hour. He’s got a couple stops ahead o’ ya, that’s all.
Good! Fine! We will be waiting for him, okay?—
—and eighteen cents is due on the shipment—
—Thank you very much! We will—we will—we will see your driver. Have a good day! Okay?—
—Have you got a—have you got a check drawn, sir?
I will be very happy to give him a check when he comes to the door!—
—I have a checkbook. Do you have a checkbook?—
—It’s taking a lot of space on our truck. What did you just say to me, sir?
Well, that’s not my problem. That’s your problem. Tell the shipper that he owes you money for taking up space on your truck!
Well we’ve been out there three times and no one answered the bell! No one answered, ah, the bell! [delay, pitch-shifted]—
—Well, isn’t that just—! So, Mr. Whip, it’s “Elmer Whip”, correct?
—[emergency siren] No, I work for United Parcel Service.
United Parcel Service! Well I’m a stockholder!—
—[repeated sirens] Three months on the job—
—So you work for me! Three months? Well, guess what, Elmer? I’m very impressed. Why don’t you give me your address and I will send you an anniversary card for being there for eighteen weeks!
Oh, would you like to come to the dock and pick it up or…? That’s an option. You know, whatever you’d like to do, sir—
—No, I would like to talk to your supervisor; how ‘bout that, Elmer Whip? Lemme talk to your supervisor.
Okay, uh, just a moment, sir.
I’m transferring you. Just a moment, sir—
—I’m here and I’ll be wa—and I will—listen, Mr. Whip, this call is being recorded—
And I am going to the police department. I’m filing a complaint. Am I clear?—
Who is this? Who is this?—
—I don’t know. Who are you calling?
I didn’t call anybody, you called me.
Uh, yes. My name’s Trip. I’m a manager at United Parcel Service. And I—
—I’ve been told, uh, by my associate that, uh, you would like to make an online payment for your COD. That is no problem. If you give me a routing number—
—It is a problem. But wha—? But wha—?
—we can accept an online payment, no problem at all! No problem at all! [siren]—
And the account number is UP-YOURS.
Ma’am, could you please be professional? [delay, pitch-shifted]
Yes, my name’s Flip Whipman. I’m a manager at UPS.com. Uh, apparently, you’ve had some—
—Oh, Flip! Great! Hey, look, I’m on a long-distance phone call. Can you give me your phone number, please, and I’ll call ya right back?
—with our driver or…? With our representative?—
—No. I haven’t had trouble with anybody. I have received every single package from UPS that I’ve ordered from other people. But again, I’m gonna log—I’m—I’m—
—Well, we’ve got a crate of magnifying glasses here that you ordered—
And who did they come from?
Good! Send it back to ‘em. I don’t want it. Okay?—
—“Send it back”? Sir, it’s a COD delivery.
That you owe $97.49 for your delivery—
Well, we’ve been out there four times—
—and no one answered the buzzer, according to my notes, here—
Well, you wanna come pick it up? That is an option. That is no problem, no problem at all! If you wanna read a credit card, we’ll leave it in a safe place!—
—I’ll drive out to UPS in Pleasantville!
If you continue calling this number—
—I am going to call the U—FCC. I am on my way to Pleasantville—to the UPS store—right now! You got a problem? Pay for it yourself! I never ordered it!
Well, I can meet ya up there. Whatever it takes to get this resolved here for ya! [delay, pitch-shifted]
